summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened a new series titled 'Jesus Over Giants' by examining the story of David and Goliath from 1 Samuel 17. He explained that giants, like the one David faced, represent the overwhelming fears and challenges in our lives—whether relational, emotional, medical, or financial. These giants try to intimidate, paralyze, and control us. The core message was that we must choose Jesus and faith over our giants and fears. Pastor Travis outlined four spiritual keys for facing giants: first, we must walk by faith and not by sight, shifting our focus from the problem to God's power. Second, we must learn to listen only to the voice of God amidst the many conflicting voices of fear, doubt, and discouragement. Third, we must remember the victories and miracles God has already brought us through in the past, as this builds faith for the current battle. Finally, we must trust God for the victory, understanding that He fights for us. The sermon concluded with a powerful call to step out in faith, just as Peter did on the water, trusting that on the other side of that step is where miracles happen. The overall tone was one of encouragement, reminding the congregation that as children of the living God, no giant is too big when faced with faith in Jesus.
mainPoints: Walk by faith and not by sight, taking your eyes off the problem and focusing on God's power.
Only listen to the voice of God, silencing the conflicting voices of fear, doubt, and discouragement.
Remember what God has already brought you through and trust Him to fight for your victory.

versesMentioned.text: On hearing the Philistine’s words, Saul and all the Israelites were dismayed and terrified.
For we live by faith, not by sight.

David asked the men standing near him, 'What will be done for the man who kills this Philistine and removes this disgrace from Israel? Who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he should defy the armies of the living God?'
David said to Saul, 'Let no one lose heart on account of this Philistine; your servant will go and fight him.'
Your servant has killed both the lion and the bear; this uncircumcised Philistine will be like one of them, because he has defied the armies of the living God.
The LORD will fight for you; you need only to be still.
Do not be afraid of them; the LORD your God himself will fight for you.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:7 (NIV) - For we live by faith, not by sight.
Monday.action: Identify one 'giant' causing you fear. Write down one truth about God's character that is bigger than that fear.
Monday.prayer: God, help me to see my situation today through the eyes of faith, not fear. Anchor my heart in Your power.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Samuel 17:26 (NIV) - ...Who is this uncircumcised Philistine that he should defy the armies of the living God?
Tuesday.action: Reframe your challenge. Instead of saying 'My giant is too big,' declare 'My God is bigger.' Speak this truth out loud.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, give me David's perspective. Remind me that any challenge defying Your purpose for me is defying You, the living God.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Samuel 17:36 (NIV) - Your servant has killed both the lion and the bear...
Wednesday.action: Take 5 minutes to write down or recall 2-3 times God has helped you through a difficult situation in the past.
Wednesday.prayer: Thank You, God, for bringing me through past battles. Let the memory of Your faithfulness build my faith for today's challenge.

Thursday.scripture: Exodus 14:14 (NIV) - The LORD will fight for you; you need only to be still.
Thursday.action: Practice 'being still' for 2 minutes today. Consciously release the need to control or figure out the solution to your giant.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I choose to be still and trust that You are fighting for me. I lay down my weapons and my worry. The battle is Yours.

Friday.scripture: Deuteronomy 3:22 (NIV) - Do not be afraid of them; the LORD your God himself will fight for you.
Friday.action: Listen to a worship song that declares God's strength and victory. Let it be the loudest voice you hear today.
Friday.prayer: God, Your Word says You Yourself fight for me. Banish fear with Your presence. Fill me with Christ-fueled courage.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 14:29 (Paraphrase) - 'Come,' he said. Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water and came toward Jesus.
Saturday.action: Identify one small, practical 'step out of the boat' you can take this weekend regarding your giant, trusting God to meet you.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, You say 'Come.' I choose to step out in faith today, believing that on the other side of this step is a miracle. Hold me up.
